title: Cacalia ferruginea
taxonomy: Cacalia ferruginea is a member of the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places Cacalia ferruginea within the larger group of flowering plants, known as angiosperms. As a member of Asteraceae, Cacalia ferruginea is further classified into the tribe Cardueae, which includes thistles and related plants. The genus Cacalia, to which Cacalia ferruginea belongs, is a small group of plants native to Asia. Cacalia ferruginea's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Asteraceae family.

intro: Cacalia ferruginea, also known as Golden Ragwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly found in eastern North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, contributing to its potential in treating various health issues, such as gastrointestinal disorders and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Cacalia ferruginea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or inflorescence, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments to support skin health and wound healing.
